perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE XIII - NOTICE Except as otherwise provided in the General Terms and Conditions applicable to this Agreement, any notice under this Agreement shall be in writing and mailed to the post office address of the Party intended to receive the same, as follows: TRANSPORTER: Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company P. O. Box 2511 Houston, Texas 77252-2511 Attention: Director, Transportation Control SHIPPER: NOTICES: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply BILLING: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply or such other address as either Party shall designate by formal written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE IV 4.1 All facilities are in place to render the service provided for in this Agreement. ARTICLE XIII - NOTICE Except as otherwise provided in the General Terms and Conditions applicable to this Agreement, any notice regarding facilities under this Agreement shall be in writing and mailed to the post office address of the Party intended to receive the same, as follows: TRANSPORTER: Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company P. O. Box 2511 Houston, Texas 77252-2511 Attention: Director, Transportation Control SHIPPER: NOTICES: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply BILLING: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply or such other address as either Party shall designate by formal written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: The Notice section is relocated within the facilities section, limiting its scope and discoverability. This change implies that the notification process is only pertinent to the facilities mentioned in Article IV and makes it difficult to enforce notifications for other breaches of contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The change contradicts KRS § 355.1.201, which defines 'notice' and establishes standards for notification within commercial agreements. By restricting the 'Notice' article to 'facilities', the amendment alters the scope of notification expected under the agreement, diminishing the effectiveness of notification about breaches unrelated to facilities. It impacts the interpretation of the commercial agreement by obscuring the intended recipients and types of notices.
perturbation.justification: The structural relocation of the notice section, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, restricts its scope and discoverability, thus affecting the overall enforceability of the contract. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' definition, specifically where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The Law Explanation also supports this by stating that the alteration diminishes the effectiveness of notification about breaches unrelated to facilities. Without provided scraped snippets, the contradiction is assessed based on the defined structural flaw and supporting explanations.
